question_text: An optician prescribes a corrective lens of power −2.0 D for a patient whose ray-optics setup is shown below. What is the focal length of the prescribed lens?

answer_key.explanation: Focal length f = 1 / P. With P = −2.0 D the focal length is −0.5 m. The negative sign tells us the lens is diverging.
answer_key.related_concept: Power and focal length of a corrective lens

visual.caption: Corrective lens before the eye.
visual.altText: Principal axis with a single distant object on the far left and a corrective lens placed between the object and a stylised eye on the right.
